text.title: model-context-protocol Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for model-context-protocol as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: MCP stdio transport requires spawning a subprocess per client connection — high connection volume causes process explosion.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each client creates its own server process. Under load, this exhausts system resources.

failure-mode.description: MCP spec is versioned; clients and servers must negotiate a compatible version — version mismatch causes silent tool registration failure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No error is raised when versions don't match; tools simply don't appear in the agent's available tool list.

failure-mode.description: Tool input schema is JSON Schema; complex recursive or polymorphic types are difficult to express and may confuse LLM tool selection.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The LLM must parse and understand the schema to select the right tool. Complex types introduce ambiguity.

failure-mode.description: MCP has no built-in authentication for stdio transport — any process with access to the transport can invoke tools.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stdio is inherently open to the local system. Use environment variables or external secrets management for sensitive credentials.

failure-mode.description: SSE transport requires a persistent HTTP connection; proxies and load balancers with short connection timeouts drop the session.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some infrastructure is configured to terminate idle connections. Monitor connection stability in production.

failure-mode.description: Error handling is limited to string error messages in tool results — structured error codes are not part of the base spec.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must parse error messages as text, which is fragile. Return consistent error message formats.

failure-mode.description: An MCP server crash does not raise an error to the agent — the agent receives a connection error on the next tool call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: MCP has no built-in health monitoring. Implement server health checks separately and alert users.
